concepts.title: Accounting Standards
concepts.description: Accounting Standards are issued by the National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) and are mandatory for all listed companies and all unlisted public companies.

concepts.title: Revaluation of Assets
concepts.description: Revaluation of assets is a change in the value of assets due to a change in the useful life of an asset or a change in the method of valuation.

concepts.title: Disclosure of Related Party Transactions
concepts.description: All related party transactions should be disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

concepts.title: Classification of Financial Statements
concepts.description: The classification of financial statements can be remembered using the mnemonic DRIP: Debt, Revenue, Income, Profit.

concepts.title: Depreciation on Revaluation Gain
concepts.description: Depreciation on revaluation gain is charged in the subsequent years and is spread over the remaining useful life of the asset.

concepts.title: Consolidated Financial Statements
concepts.description: Consolidated financial statements combine the financial statements of a parent company and its subsidiaries.

concepts.title: Going Concern
concepts.description: Going concern is an assumption that a company will continue to operate for the foreseeable future.

concepts.title: Dividend on Share Capital
concepts.description: Dividend on share capital is a distribution of profit to shareholders and is usually declared out of the company's retained earnings.
